column5: Tasks
column6: Dataset Description

column5: Text Classification,  Emotion Analysis
column6: Leyzer is a multilingual text corpus designed for natural language understanding (NLU) of virtual assistants, covering 20 domains and 186 intents across English, Spanish, and Polish. Each intent contains 1¨C672 sentences, and it supports research on cross-lingual transfer learning and localization strategies. The dataset is officially presented in the paper published at TSD 2020 (DOI: 10.1007/978-3-030-58323-1_51) and constructed with computer-assisted translation tools.

column5: Question Answering, Information Retrieval
column6: MAUPQA is a human-annotated Polish dataset for open-domain question answering and passage retrieval. Collected from Polish texts and annotated by native speakers, it is split into train, validation, and test sets. It supports evaluating dense retrieval and QA models, and is publicly available on Hugging Face Datasets.

column5: Information Retrieval, Semantic Similarity, Cross-lingual Transfer
column6: mFAQ is a multilingual FAQ retrieval dataset (21 languages) built from web-scraped FAQ pages (no human annotators). It has ~6M FAQ pairs split into train/val/test sets (no exact per-split counts). Released for academic research (multilingual/cross-lingual FAQ retrieval), it allows free non-commercial use with no strict usage restrictions.
column10: The dataset URL is accessible via GitHub.

column5: Emotion Recognition,  Affective Computing, Psycholinguistic Research
column6: A standardized database of 1,356 realistic, high-quality photographs with normative ratings for valence, arousal, and approach-avoidance dimensions, categorized into people, faces, animals, objects, and landscapes.
column10: Original official URL is no longer accessible.

column5: Sentiment Analysis
column6: A sentiment classification dataset consisting of user reviews from the Polish e-commerce platform Allegro, used in the KLEJ benchmark.

column5: Natural Language Understanding, Coreference Resolution, Syntactic Analysis
column6: A manually annotated corpus of Polish texts with coreference relations, designed to support research in coreference resolution for Polish.

column5: Text Classification, Text Embedding Evaluation, Semantic Similarity, Clustering
column6: PL-MTEB (Polish Massive Text Embedding Benchmark) is a comprehensive benchmark suite for evaluating text embedding models in the Polish language, consisting of 28 diverse NLP tasks across five categories including classification, clustering, pair classification, information retrieval, and semantic textual similarity. The benchmark incorporates previously used Polish datasets and adds a new PLSC (Polish Library of Science Corpus) for clustering tasks, providing open-source code and evaluation protocols.

column5: Word Sense Disambiguation, Lexical Semantics, Natural Language Understanding,  Knowledge Base Population
column6: plWordNet is a large-scale lexical-semantic network for the Polish language, serving as the Polish counterpart to Princeton WordNet. It contains 285,000 word meanings (synsets) interconnected by over 600,000 semantic relations (e.g., synonymy, hyponymy). Each word meaning is assigned a unique identifier, linked to lemmas, part-of-speech tags, and detailed semantic descriptions. It supports direct mapping to Princeton WordNet (over 300,000 cross-lingual relations) and is extended with emotion annotations (valence, arousal, 8 basic emotions) for 6000 word meanings.

column5: Emotion Analysis, Sentiment Analysis, Affective Computing
column6: The Emotion Meanings dataset is a lexicon of 6000 Polish word meanings derived from the Polish plWordNet, where each meaning is annotated with valence, arousal, and basic emotion category norms, designed for affective research in psychology, computational linguistics, and language technologies.

column5: Automatic Speech Recognition, Multimodal Understanding, Language Acquisition Analysis
column6: The POLEMAD dataset is a multimodal speech database of Polish pronunciation that includes synchronized electromagnetic articulography (EMA) recordings, high-quality multichannel audio, and video data collected from multiple speakers, designed to support research on articulatory modeling, speech production analysis, and multimodal speech processing.
column10: The POLEMAD dataset is described in the cited paper, but no publicly accessible download repository or direct dataset URL is provided.

column5: Sentiment Analysis, Text Classification, Emotion Classification
column6: A manually annotated corpus of Polish consumer reviews from four domains (medicine, hotels, products, school) for sentiment analysis at document and sentence levels.

column5: Word Sense Disambiguation, Lexical Semantics, Metaphor Detection
column6: The PolEval 2017 corpus is a manually annotated Polish language dataset released for the PolEval shared tasks, containing linguistically labeled text (e.g., morphological and syntactic annotations) designed to support evaluation and development of core NLP tasks such as part-of-speech tagging and parsing.

column1: Affective Norms for 718 Polish Short Texts
column5: Affective Computing, Emotion Analysis
column6: This dataset provides standardized ratings for 718 Polish short texts (sentences, phrases) across multiple affective and psycholinguistic dimensions, including valence, arousal, dominance, origin, and subjective significance. It is designed to serve as standardized stimulus material for emotional, cognitive, and psycholinguistic experiments using Polish text materials.

column5: Sentiment Analysis, Text Classification, Emotion Classification, Affective Computing
column6: A sentiment analysis dataset of Polish product reviews extracted from the e-commerce marketplace Allegro.pl, with ratings on a 1-5 scale.
column10: The citing paper cannot be accessed due to copyright restrictions.

column5: Emotion Recognition
column6: A revised dataset of affective norms for Polish words providing emotional ratings across multiple dimensions.

column5: Hate Speech Classification, Offensive Language Detection
column6: A Polish language dataset of banned offensive posts and comments from the Wykop.pl social media service, labeled as harmful or neutral.

column5: Historical Linguistics, Text Classification, Language Modeling, Syntactic Analysis
column6: A large, morphosyntactically annotated historical corpus of Polish texts from the 17th and 18th centuries (up to 1772), comprising approximately 13.5 million segments.

column5: Information Retrieval, Question Answering, Text Classification, Semantic Similarity
column6: a new benchmark which comprises 13 datasets, facilitating further development, training and evaluation of modern Polish language models for IR tasks.

column1: Creative Commons from Common Crawl Corpus
column5: Language Modeling, Word Embeddings, Semantic Similarity, Information Retrieval
column6: A multilingual web-scale corpus extracted from Common Crawl consisting of Creative Commons licensed documents.
column10: The cited paper introduces the C4Corpus dataset but does not provide a direct dataset download URL.

column5: Text Classification, Hate Speech Classification, Toxicity Detection
column6: A curated online catalogue of datasets related to abusive and hateful language research.

column5: Text Classification, Toxicity Detection
column6: A dataset of Polish Twitter messages annotated for harmful content, including cyberbullying and hate speech, released for the PolEval 2019 Shared Task on automatic cyberbullying detection.

column5: Semantic Similarity, Compositional Semantics Modeling, Textual Entailment, Natural Language Understanding
column6: A corpus of sentence pairs annotated for semantic relatedness and textual entailment to evaluate compositional distributional semantics models.

column5: Question Answering, Information Retrieval, Text Classification, Language Modeling
column6: A Polish question-answering dataset containing questions from the ˇ°Did you know?ˇ± section of Polish Wikipedia linked to relevant articles.

column6: A major release of the OpenSubtitles collection of parallel corpora, compiled from a large database of movie and TV subtitles.

column5: Question Answering, Reading Comprehension, Information Retrieval
column6: an open-domain question answering evaluation set comprising 10k question-answer pairs aligned across 26 typologically diverse languages

column5: Text Classification, Sentiment Analysis, Hate Speech Classification, Information Extraction
column6: A corpus of Polish online forum posts annotated for offensive content and hate speech detection.

column5: Word Embeddings, Pre-training, Text Classification
column6: A large-scale corpus of Polish text (1.35 billion tokens) compiled from diverse web sources and used for training word embeddings.

column5: Natural Language Understanding, Text Classification
column6: A comprehensive multi-task benchmark for evaluating Polish language understanding, consisting of nine diverse tasks such as sentiment analysis, named entity recognition, and question answering.

column5: Aspect Sentiment Triplet Extraction
column6: A Polish annotated dataset for aspect-level sentiment triple extraction.

column5: Lexical Semantics
column6: A Polish lexical dictionary providing synonym relations between words, used as a reference resource for synonym replacement and lexical normalization.

column5: Text Classification, Language Modeling, Information Retrieval
column6: A large corpus of transcripts from the proceedings of the Polish Parliament (Sejm), designed for linguistic and computational research.

column5: Psycholinguistic Research
column6: Two sets of algorithmically constructed Polish pseudowords (pseudonouns and pseudoverbs) that are orthographically and phonologically plausible, designed for psycholinguistic research on grammatical class processing.

column5: Cross-lingual Transfer, Machine Translation, Language Modeling
column6: The Polish portion of the OpenSubtitles2018 corpus, a large-scale parallel corpus extracted from movie and TV subtitles, widely used for machine translation and cross-lingual research

column5: Text summarization
column6: A corpus of Polish news summaries created to support the development and evaluation of automated single-document summarization tools. It includes both abstractive and extractive summaries

column5: Lexical Semantics, Semantic Similarity, Word Sense Disambiguation
column6: The Polish WordNet (plWordNet) integrated within the Multilingual Central Repository (MCR), a large-scale lexical-semantic knowledge base aligned with Princeton WordNet.

column5: Text Classification, Sentiment Analysis
column6: A dataset of political advertisements collected from Twitter, annotated with information such as advertiser, target audience, and sentiment, used for analyzing political communication and framing.

column5: Question Answering, Reading Comprehension
column6: The first Polish open-domain question answering (OpenQA) dataset. It consists of 7Ł¬000 questionsŁ¬ 87Ł¬525 manually labeled evidence passagesŁ¬ and a corpus of over 7 million candidate passages from Polish Wikipedia

column5: Text summarization
column6: A corpus created to support the development and evaluation of tools for automated single-document summarization of Polish. It contains a large number of manual summaries of news articles.

column5: Multimodal Understanding, Visual Question Answering
column6: The first Polish large-scale Visual Question Answering (VQA) dataset. It contains over 40k questions in Polish about images from the Visual Genome dataset, designed to advance multimodal AI research for Polish.

column5: Text summarization, Language Modeling
column6: A collection of articles from the Web archive of ˇ°Rzeczpospolitaˇ±, a nationwide Polish daily newspaper (1993-2002). It served as the original text source for the creation of the Polish Summaries Corpus

column5: Sentiment Analysis, Natural Language Understanding, Information Retrieval
column6: A collection of datasets from the SemEval-2023 workshop, which includes multiple shared tasks (e.g., sentiment analysis, irony detection, multilingual complex NLI) aimed at advancing the state of the art in semantic analysis.

column5: Language Modeling, Text Embedding Evaluation
column6: An open-source, continuously updated, large-scale corpus of Polish text from diverse sources, designed for training and evaluating Polish language models. It also provides data processing tools.

column5: Word Embeddings, Text Embedding Evaluation
column6: A set of pre-trained Word2Vec models for Polish, trained on large corpora including web-crawled data, made available through the CLARIN-PL repository.

column5: Psychological Research
column6: A set of 299 standardized pictures with Polish norms for name agreement, visual complexity, and concept familiarity, used as stimuli in psycholinguistic picture naming tasks.
